prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "后踢腿跑" (butt kicks / heel-up running drill) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person performing a butt-kick running drill at the heel-up phase: right leg supporting on the ball of the foot in mid-stride contact; left knee bent so that the left heel kicks up to touch the left buttock (left foot pulled up to the glute), the left thigh remains roughly vertical (perpendicular to the floor); arms in running-style opposition; torso upright with slight forward lean; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hamstring group of the kicking (left) leg (heel-to-glute action). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: calf muscles of the supporting leg and gluteus maximus. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group of the kicking leg; "小腿三头肌" pointing to the calf muscles of the supporting leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
